His [Haydn piano sonatas](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=1b7630d6dc96d54f872e14c72b60f1fe803df002cfd7f777a69319c02110ea0d&blog_id=42693868&post_id=90913&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=51863ed490be3a84c0a09bf3bfa1f336&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=e8cb6b7e778a4ceea5dd5d3940412a78) were his best work and also they were pathbreaking in helping us understand that composer. I also very much appreciated his short Beethoven pieces on the old Vox box set, starting with the Bagatelles, and as a live performer his Schubert was memorable...
|
